page: Microsoft Azure FX12mds_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 12 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 6 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The server is equipped with 252.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a 945 GB disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPUs are included.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core scores and text processing benchmarks reaching the top 10% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show strong performance in the top 25% tier. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth is measured at 1035.91 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 94.39 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the server achieves a top-tier text generation speed of 398.8 tokens/sec using a small 135M parameter model, though performance scales down to an average of 11.89 tokens/sec for a medium 7B model.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the FX12mds_v2 offers high memory density and dedicated high-frequency CPU cores, making it suitable for workloads requiring substantial RAM without needing massive parallel core counts. The combination of 252.0 GB of memory, a 945 GB local disk, and strong single-threaded performance makes this instance type appropriate for memory-intensive databases, text processing pipelines, software compilation, and caching layers like Redis, which registered 19.38 million operations per second in benchmark testing.
description: The Microsoft Azure FX12mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 12 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with Hyper-V virtualization, it delivers 252.0 GB of RAM (21.0 GB per core) and includes a 945 GB local disk. The processor features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores in the top 10% tier, alongside strong single-threaded decompression. Multi-core and database benchmarks perform in the average tier, with Redis SET operations reaching 19.38 million operations per second. This instance provides high memory density relative to its core count, making it highly suitable for memory-intensive databases, text processing, and caching workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX12mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 252 GB of RAM, and a 945 GB local disk, delivering top-tier single-core performance.

tagline: A high-frequency, memory-intensive virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores, massive RAM density, and integrated local storage for demanding workloads.
bullet_points: 12 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
252.0 GB of RAM providing a high 21.0 GB per core resource density
945 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core and text processing benchmark performance
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, caching, and text processing
